title: Alkanes
summary: 9701 AS alkane chemistry — formulae, free-radical substitution, combustion, and cracking with PhET molecule builder.
learningObjectives: Write general and structural formulae for alkanes.
Describe free-radical substitution mechanism for halogenation.
Balance combustion equations and predict incomplete combustion products.
Explain catalytic and thermal cracking of long-chain alkanes.

simpleExplanation.title: Saturated hydrocarbons
simpleExplanation.summary: Alkanes contain only C–C and C–H single bonds. They undergo substitution (not addition) and burn to release energy.
steps: General formula CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ — homologous series: CH₄, C₂H₆, C₃H₈…
Free-radical substitution: Cl₂ + UV → Cl•; chain propagation steps.
Complete combustion: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O.

sections.content: **Alkanes** (14.1) link to **alkenes** (14.2) and **halogenoalkanes** (15.1). Examiners test **mechanism arrows** for chlorination and **environmental** aspects of incomplete combustion.
